page: Amazon Web Services c5n.xlarge is a compute-optimized, network-optimized, and EBS-optimized virtual machine instance running on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8124M processor operating at 3.4 GHz. The instance provides 4 virtual CPUs, which correspond to 2 physical cores with 2 threads per core. It is equipped with 10.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, yielding 2.62 GB of memory per core. This instance does not include any local storage or GPU accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 5 Gbps.

In benchmark testing, the Intel Xeon processor delivers average single-core performance, as evidenced by a stress-ng single-core CPU score of 1406.1 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1133.0. Single-threaded tasks such as gzip compression (23.76 GB/sec) and bzip3 decompression (9.01 GB/sec) also fall within the average range. However, multi-core and memory-bound operations show limitations. The multi-core stress-ng CPU performance is rated as weak at 3689.06, and the Geekbench multi-core score is 2509.0. Memory bandwidth benchmarks are consistently weak, with read-only large block transfers at 25.89 GB/sec and write large block transfers at 23.1 GB/sec. Passmark memory testing registers a poor score of 1031.78.

The hardware profile of the c5n.xlarge represents a specific tradeoff, prioritizing network optimization and dedicated compute cores over memory capacity and local storage. While the instance offers dedicated CPU resources and AVX-512 instruction support, its low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.5 GB per vCPU and weak multi-threaded scaling limit its utility for memory-intensive workloads. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, this instance is suited for network-bound workloads that require dedicated CPU allocation but do not demand massive memory footprints or local disk I/O. Appropriate workloads include lightweight web serving, basic network appliances, single-threaded data processing, and moderate utility computing tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services c5n.xlarge is a compute-optimized and network-optimized instance featuring 4 vCPUs and 10.0 GB of DDR4 memory. Powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8124M processor running at 3.4 GHz on the Nitro hypervisor, this x86_64 instance provides 2 physical cores with hyper-threading. It lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying on a 5 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data indicates average single-core performance, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1406.1 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1133.0. However, multi-core and memory bandwidth benchmarks are weak, with large-block memory read speeds at 25.89 GB/sec and a Passmark memory score of 1031.78. Given its dedicated CPU allocation and low memory-to-vCPU ratio, the c5n.xlarge is best suited for network-bound workloads, lightweight web serving, and single-threaded processing tasks rather than memory-intensive or highly parallelized applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c5n.xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 4 vCPUs, 10.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, and a 3.4 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, designed for network-bound workloads requiring dedicated compute resources.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c5n.xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 4 vCPUs, 10.0 GB DDR4 RAM, and 5 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
tagline: A network-optimized compute instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and balanced single-core performance for network-bound workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 8124M processor with 4 vCPUs and 2 physical cores
10.0 GB of DDR4 memory with a 5 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Average single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in the hardware profile
Best suited for network-bound applications and lightweight web serving
